revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Saruga Jinja
猿賀神社 | image = Saruga Shrine torii.jpeg | width = | caption = [[Torii]] on the approach to Saruka Jinja | type = | dedication = | founded = 807 | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]] | phone = | website = }} {{nihongo|'''Saruka Jinja'''|猿賀神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Said to have been founded in 807, there is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010100600077/ |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturaly-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012}} ==See also== {{Commons category|Saruga shrine}} * [[Seito shoin teien]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirakawa, Aomori]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} [[ja:猿賀神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Saruga Jinja

Said to have been founded in 807, there is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010100600077/ |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012}} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} ==See also== {{Commons category|Saruga shrine}} * [[Seito shoin teien]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirakawa, Aomori]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} [[ja:猿賀神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Saruka Jinja

The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012}} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} There is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. ==See also== {{Commons category|Saruga shrine}} * [[Seito shoin teien]] * [[Seibi-en]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirakawa, Aomori]] [[Category:807 establishments]] {{Japan-struct-stub}} [[ja:猿賀神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Saruka Jinja

The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012}} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} There is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). ==See also== {{Commons category|Saruga shrine}} * [[Seito shoin teien]] * [[Seibi-en]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|40|37|01|N|140|33|47|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirakawa, Aomori]] [[Category:807 establishments]] {{Japan-struct-stub}}

The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100910122333/http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |archivedate=10 September 2010 |df= }} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120519235826/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |archivedate=19 May 2012 |df= }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). [[File:Saruga Shrine haiden.jpeg|thumb|left|The ''[[haiden]]'' of Saruka Shrine]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Saruga shrine}} * [[Seito shoin teien]] * [[Seibi-en]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirakawa, Aomori]] [[Category:807 establishments]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] {{Japan-struct-stub}}

The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100910122333/http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |archivedate=10 September 2010 }} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120519235826/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |archivedate=19 May 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). [[File:Saruga Shrine haiden.jpeg|thumb|left|The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' of Saruka Shrine]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Saruga shrine}} * [[Seito shoin teien]] * [[Seibi-en]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirakawa, Aomori]] [[Category:807 establishments]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 807]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

猿賀神社 | image = Saruga Shrine torii.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] on the approach to Saruka Jinja | map_type = Japan Aomori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|40|37|01|N|140|33|47|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 807 | date_destroyed = | location = [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Saruka Jinja'''|猿賀神社}}, sometimes rendered '''Saruga''', is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=21 May 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170521212556/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |url-status=dead }}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304032204/http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |url-status=dead }} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100910122333/http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |archivedate=10 September 2010 }} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120519235826/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |archivedate=19 May 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). [[File:Saruga Shrine haiden.jpeg|thumb|left|The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' of Saruka Shrine]] ==See also== {{Commons category|Saruga shrine}} * [[Seito shoin teien]] * [[Seibi-en]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Hirakawa, Aomori]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:807 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 800s]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Saruka Shrine]][[Category:Aomori Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia06]] [[en:Saruka Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia06]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia06]] [[en:Saruka Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia06]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia26]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia22]]

猿賀神社 | image = Saruga Shrine torii.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] on the approach to Saruka Jinja | map_type = Japan Aomori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|40|37|01|N|140|33|47|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 807 | date_destroyed = | location = [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Saruka Jinja'''|猿賀神社}}, sometimes rendered '''Saruga''', is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=21 May 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170521212556/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |url-status=dead }}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304032204/http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |url-status=dead }} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100910122333/http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |archivedate=10 September 2010 }} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120519235826/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |archivedate=19 May 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). 猿賀神社 | image = Saruga Shrine torii.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] on the approach to Saruka Jinja | map_type = Japan Aomori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|40|37|01|N|140|33|47|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 807 | date_destroyed = | location = [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Saruka Jinja'''|猿賀神社}}, sometimes rendered '''Saruga''', is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=21 May 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170521212556/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |url-status=dead }}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304032204/http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |url-status=dead }} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100910122333/http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |archivedate=10 September 2010 }} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120519235826/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |archivedate=19 May 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Saruka Jinja
猿賀神社 | image = Saruga Shrine torii.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] on the approach to Saruka Jinja | map_type = Japan Aomori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|40|37|01|N|140|33|47|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 807 | date_destroyed = | location = [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Saruka Jinja'''|猿賀神社}}, sometimes rendered '''Saruga''', is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[Aomori Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=21 May 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170521212556/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |url-status=dead }}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304032204/http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |url-status=dead }} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100910122333/http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |archivedate=10 September 2010 }} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120519235826/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |archivedate=19 May 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Matsuri|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). 猿賀神社 | image = Saruga Shrine torii.jpeg | caption = [[Torii]] on the approach to Saruka Jinja | map_type = Japan Aomori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|40|37|01|N|140|33|47|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 807 | date_destroyed = | location = [[:en:Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Saruka Jinja'''|猿賀神社}}, sometimes rendered '''Saruga''', is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa]], [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[:en:Ezo|Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[:en:Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=21 May 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170521212556/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |url-status=dead }}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304032204/http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |url-status=dead }} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |url=http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |title=猿賀神社 |publisher=Aomori Jinjachō |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100910122333/http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |archivedate=10 September 2010 }} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[:en:Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120519235826/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |archivedate=19 May 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Japanese festivals|festival]] and a pond of pink [[Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). [[File:Saruga Shrine haiden.jpeg|thumb|left|The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' of Saruka Shrine]] ==See also== {{Commons Category|Saruga shrine}} * [[Seito shoin teien]] * [[Seibi-en]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} ]] ]] ]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:807 establishments]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in the 800s]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Aomori Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] }} [[sn:Saruka Shrine]] [[en:Saruka Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ساروكا جينجا]] {{Commons category|Saruga shrine}} [[fr:Saruka-jinja]] [[ja:猿賀神社]] [[mr:सारुका जिंजा]]

The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the [[:en:Ezo|Ezo]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher=[[:en:Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=21 May 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170521212556/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |url-status=dead }}{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |title=Saruka Jinja |publisher=[[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304032204/http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/nourin/noson/files/0265sarukajinjya.pdf |url-status=dead }} The [[Honden]], dating to 1826, is a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|Prefecturally-designated Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher=[[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] |accessdate=10 February 2012}}{{cite web |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100910122333/http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/nankoku/jinja/48.htm |archivedate=10 September 2010 }} Preserved {{nihongo|building records|棟札}} are a [[Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan#Designated Tangible Cultural Properties|City-designated Important Cultural Property (Historic Materials)]].{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |title=指定文化財一覧 (Hirakawa Cultural Property Listing) |publisher=[[:en:Hirakawa, Aomori|Hirakawa City]] |accessdate=10 February 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120519235826/http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/docs/2010110900013/ |archivedate=19 May 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Japanese festivals|festival]] and a pond of pink [[:en:Nelumbo nucifera|lotus]]. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). The shrine is said to have been founded in 807, when [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] came north during the campaigns against the {{ill|Ezo|en|Ezo|WD=Q1040184|lt=Ezo}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.hirakawa.lg.jp/english/2010112500303/ |title=Saruka Shrine |publisher={{ill|Hirakawa, Aomori|en|Hirakawa, Aomori|WD=Q695930|lt=Hirakawa City}} |accessdate=10 February 2012 The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命). 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Japanese festivals|festival]] and a pond of pink {{ill|Nelumbo nucifera|en|Nelumbo nucifera|WD=Q16528|lt=lotus|1=Nelumbo nucifera}}. |url=http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/juho_kenzoubutu_27.html |title=Saruka Jinja Honden |publisher={{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori 2012 }} There is an annual rice-planting [[Japanese festivals|festival]] and a pond of pink {{ill|Nelumbo nucifera|en|Nelumbo nucifera|WD=Q16528|lt=lotus|1=Nelumbo nucifera}}. The main [[kami]] enshrined here is Kamitsukenokimitaji no mikoto (上毛野君田道命).
